description: Replace the unreliable proactive extension reload mechanism with a content-hash-based approach. The build scripts compute a SHA256 hash of the extension bundle files and write it into manifest.json as a custom field. The server reads this hash from the installed extension and sends it in sync.full. The side panel compares the server's hash against its own (from chrome.runtime.getManifest()) and shows a retro-themed dialog prompting the user to reload when a mismatch is detected. This works in both production (version bump) and dev mode (any rebuild). The old version-change proactive reload paths (pendingExtensionReload, sendExtensionReload on version change) are removed.

userStories.title: Compute extension content hash at build time and write to manifest.json
userStories.description: Add a post-build step that computes a SHA256 hash of the key extension bundle files and writes it as an extensionHash field in manifest.json. This hash changes whenever any bundle output changes, even without a version bump.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json contains an extensionHash field after npm run build
The hash is a 16-character hex string (first 8 bytes of SHA256)

The hash is computed from the concatenated contents of dist/background.js, dist/side-panel/side-panel.js, dist/side-panel/styles.css, dist/offscreen/index.js, and manifest.json (without the extensionHash field itself)
Two consecutive builds with identical source produce the same hash
Changing any source file produces a different hash after rebuild

userStories.title: Send extensionHash in sync.full from the MCP server
userStories.description: The MCP server reads the extensionHash from the installed extension's manifest.json at ~/.opentabs/extension/manifest.json and includes it in the sync.full payload and ConfigStateResult so the side panel can compare it against its own hash.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStateResult in platform/shared/src/index.ts has an extensionHash optional string field
buildConfigStatePayload reads extensionHash from the installed manifest.json and includes it in the payload
sync.full params include extensionHash
The side panel receives extensionHash via getFullState and stores it in state
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/shared/src/index.ts (add extensionHash to ConfigStateResult)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (read hash, include in payload)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-install.ts (optionally cache the hash after install)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (add extensionHash to FullStateResult)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (add extensionHash state)
- platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.ts (add extensionHash to cache interface)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (extract extensionHash in handleSyncFull)
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (return extensionHash in getFullState)

Context:
The serverVersion field already flows through this exact pipeline. extensionHash follows the same path:
1. buildConfigStatePayload (extension-handlers.ts:346-397) builds the payload — add extensionHash here
2. sendSyncFull (extension-protocol.ts:105-148) sends it in params
3. handleSyncFull (message-router.ts:345-510) extracts it at line ~463 and calls updateServerStateCache
4. server-state-cache.ts stores it in the ServerStateCache interface
5. handleBgGetFullState (background-message-handlers.ts:146-273) returns it at line ~258
6. bridge.ts FullStateResult type includes it
7. App.tsx receives it via getFullState and stores in useState

Changes:

1. In `platform/shared/src/index.ts` (line ~381), add to ConfigStateResult:
```typescript
/** Content hash of the installed extension bundle files — used by the side panel to detect stale extension code */
extensionHash?: string;
```

2. In `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts`, the buildConfigStatePayload function (line 346-397):
- Import `readFileSync` and `join` from node builtins
- Import `getExtensionDir` from `./config.js`
- Read the extensionHash from `~/.opentabs/extension/manifest.json`:
```typescript
let extensionHash: string | undefined;
try {
  const manifestPath = join(getExtensionDir(), 'manifest.json');
  const manifest = JSON.parse(readFileSync(manifestPath, 'utf-8'));
  extensionHash = typeof manifest.extensionHash === 'string' ? manifest.extensionHash : undefined;
} catch {
  // Extension manifest not readable — skip hash
}
```
- Add `extensionHash` to the returned object (alongside `serverVersion` at line 393)

3. In `platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.ts`, add `extensionHash?: string` to the `ServerStateCache` interface (around line 25).

4. In `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` handleSyncFull (around line 463), extract extensionHash alongside serverVersion:
```typescript
const rawExtensionHash = typeof params.extensionHash === 'string' ? params.extensionHash : undefined;
```
And include it in the `updateServerStateCache` call.

5. In `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts` handleBgGetFullState (around line 258), add:
```typescript
extensionHash: serverCache.extensionHash,
```

6. In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts`, add `extensionHash?: string` to the `FullStateResult` interface.

7. In `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx`:
- Add state: `const [extensionHash, setExtensionHash] = useState<string | undefined>(undefined);`
- In `applyFullState`, set it: `setExtensionHash(result.extensionHash);`
- On disconnect (line ~335), clear it: `setExtensionHash(undefined);`

Constraints:
- Read the manifest synchronously (readFileSync) since buildConfigStatePayload is called frequently and should be fast.
- If the manifest doesn't exist or extensionHash is missing, return undefined — don't fail.
- Do NOT cache the hash in ServerState — always read from disk so hot-reloads pick up changes immediately.
- Follow the exact same pattern as serverVersion for all plumbing.

userStories.title: Add ExtensionUpdateDialog component with retro-themed reload prompt
userStories.description: Create a new side panel component that shows a blocking dialog when the extension hash from the server doesn't match the running extension's hash. The dialog uses the retro design system and has a Reload button that calls chrome.runtime.reload().
acceptanceCriteria: ExtensionUpdateDialog component renders a retro Dialog when extensionHash from server differs from chrome.runtime.getManifest().extensionHash
Dialog has a yellow header reading 'Extension Updated'
Dialog body explains that a new version has been installed and the extension needs to reload
Dialog has a single 'Reload' button that calls chrome.runtime.reload()
Dialog cannot be dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape (modal, blocking)
Dialog does not appear when extensionHash is undefined (server hasn't sent it yet) or when hashes match
Dialog does not appear when not connected to the server
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ExtensionUpdateDialog.tsx (new file) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (render the dialog) Context: The retro Dialog component is at `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Dialog.tsx`. It uses Radix UI Dialog with retro styling: `rounded border-2 border-border bg-background shadow-md`, header with `bg-primary text-primary-foreground`, and fade/zoom animations. The unreviewed plugin confirmation dialog in PluginCard.tsx (lines 343-367) is the best reference pattern. It uses `onInteractOutside={(e: Event) => e.preventDefault()}` to block dismissal. The side panel design system uses: - `font-head` (Archivo Black) for headers - `font-sans` (Space Grotesk) for body text - `font-mono` (Space Mono) for code/technical text - `bg-primary` (#ffdb33) for the dialog header bar - `text-foreground` for body text - `text-muted-foreground` for secondary text - Retro Button component for actions Changes: 1. Create `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ExtensionUpdateDialog.tsx`: ```tsx import { RefreshCw } from 'lucide-react'; import { Dialog } from './retro/Dialog'; import { Button } from './retro/Button'; interface ExtensionUpdateDialogProps { open: boolean; } const ExtensionUpdateDialog = ({ open }: ExtensionUpdateDialogProps) => ( e.preventDefault()} onEscapeKeyDown={(e: Event) => e.preventDefault()}> Extension Updated

A new version of the extension has been installed. Reload to apply the update.

); export { ExtensionUpdateDialog }; ``` Note on the Dialog.Header: The retro Dialog.Header component already includes a close X button (see retro/Dialog.tsx line 50-52). Since this dialog should NOT be dismissable, you need to either: - Override the Header to hide the X button (add a `closable` prop to Dialog.Header) - Or don't use Dialog.Header and create a custom header div with the same styling but no X button - The better approach: Use `onOpenChange` on the Dialog root to prevent closing: ` {}}>`. And for the X button in the header, you can use a custom header div instead of Dialog.Header to avoid the built-in close button. Alternative header without X button: ```tsx
Extension Updated
``` 2. In App.tsx, determine when to show the dialog. Add this logic: ```tsx const runningHash = chrome.runtime.getManifest().extensionHash; const hashMismatch = connected && extensionHash !== undefined && runningHash !== undefined && extensionHash !== runningHash; ``` Then render: `` Place it at the top level of the App return, outside other content, so it overlays everything. Constraints: - The dialog MUST use the retro design system — same border, shadow, colors as other dialogs. - The dialog MUST NOT be dismissable (no X button, no click-outside, no Escape key). - The dialog should only appear when: connected AND extensionHash from server is defined AND differs from running manifest's extensionHash. - `chrome.runtime.getManifest()` returns the manifest.json of the currently running extension — this is what Chrome loaded. After files are updated on disk, this still returns the OLD manifest until reload. - The `extensionHash` field in the manifest is a custom field. TypeScript will need a cast: `(chrome.runtime.getManifest() as { extensionHash?: string }).extensionHash`. - Do NOT auto-reload. Show the dialog and let the user click Reload. - The RefreshCw icon from lucide-react is already used elsewhere in the codebase.

userStories.title: Remove proactive version-change extension reload paths
userStories.description: Remove the pendingExtensionReload flag and the version-change-triggered sendExtensionReload calls from the MCP server. The side panel hash check replaces this mechanism. Keep the auth secret rotation reload and the extension_reload MCP tool.
acceptanceCriteria: pendingExtensionReload field is removed from ServerState interface and createState
The version-change block in performReload (reload.ts lines 480-497) no longer calls sendExtensionReload or sets pendingExtensionReload
The deferred reload check in http-routes.ts (lines 1067-1077) is removed
The auth secret rotation reload in reload.ts (lines 402-414) is preserved unchanged
The extension_reload MCP tool in browser-tools/reload-extension.ts is preserved unchanged
The handleExtensionReload handler in message-router.ts is preserved unchanged
sendExtensionReload function is preserved (still used by auth secret rotation)

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for extension hash mismatch detection and reload dialog
userStories.description: Add comprehensive E2E tests covering the extension hash mismatch detection, the reload dialog UI, and both production and dev mode scenarios. Include stress tests for rapid reconnection cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test verifies the ExtensionUpdateDialog appears when extensionHash from the server differs from the running extension's hash
E2E test verifies the dialog does NOT appear when hashes match
E2E test verifies the dialog cannot be dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape
E2E test verifies the Reload button is present and clickable
E2E test simulates a production update scenario: server sends a different extensionHash, dialog appears
E2E test simulates a dev mode rebuild scenario: extension files change, server sends updated hash, dialog appears
Stress test: rapid connect/disconnect cycles do not cause spurious dialog appearances or crashes
E2E test verifies the old proactive reload path no longer sends extension.reload on version mismatch
